{{Coordinates list}} '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群) is a group of ancient tombs ({{ill|Kofun Group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Shijō Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|12=draft|12=draft}}. As of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|12=draft|12=draft}}), 14 tumuli have been confirmed; however, all have been destroyed by later generations and exist only as buried tombs with no surviving mounds on the surface. == Overview == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群)}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Suizei Tomb|lt_ja=現・綏靖陵|12=draft|12=draft}}}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Jimmu Tomb|lt_ja=現・神武陵|12=draft|12=draft}}}} {{Center|{{Smaller|(Estimated location within {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft}})}}}} {{Image label end}} Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}} and near the intersection of Shijō Town and the northeast side of {{ill|Unebayama|ja|畝傍山|12=draft|12=draft}}, this kofun group was constructed from the early part of the middle Kofun period to the early part of the late Kofun period (from the first half of the 5th century to the mid-6th century){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. The entire area lies within the bounds of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft}}, and it is said that at the end of the 7th century, with the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, the mounds were leveled and the moats were filled in{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In {{ill|1987|ja|1987|12=draft|12=draft}} (Showa 62), the first tumulus (Tumulus No.1) was discovered during the initial excavation survey; subsequently, due to investigations required for the construction of the {{ill|Yamato Takada Bypass|ja|大和高田バイパス|12=draft|12=draft}}, a total of 31 surveys were conducted, resulting in the discovery of 14 tumuli as of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|12=draft|12=draft}}){{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}. Although the burial facilities are unclear due to the leveling of the mounds, many wooden artifacts have been found in excellent condition from the moats – presumably because the filled-in moats retained moisture{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}. In particular, Tumulus No.1 has revealed its entire structure as a square tumulus with a built‐in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|12=draft|12=draft}}) component, with numerous haniwa and wooden artifacts recovered{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In addition to the tumuli, settlement traces from the Kofun period were also discovered, and together they are collectively referred to as the "Shijō Iseki"{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Near the kofun group lie the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Misanzai) and the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tsukayama), suggesting a possible relationship between these early imperial tombs and the kofun group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. == List == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Shijō Kofun Group (Bold indicates principal tumulus) {{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} ! order of Tumulus No.7 → No.2 → No.1, and that the peak period of the kofun group was from the latter half to the end of the middle period{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|Tumulus No.6 – House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Explanatory sign 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Wooden artifact in the shape of a bird
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|Tumulus No.14 (Excavation survey in 2019) == Tumulus No.1 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus No.1}}{{small|Photo taken during the 2017 excavation survey.}}]] '''Tumulus No.1''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. It was the first tumulus discovered within the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}} ({{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}), and excavation surveys by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}} and others have been conducted since {{ill|1987|ja|1987|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the mound was destroyed and its fill lost during the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, it originally measured approximately 32 meters north–south and 40 meters east–west (including the built-in structure){{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. On the western side of the mound, a trapezoidal built-in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|12=draft|12=draft}}) measuring 9 meters in length and 14 meters in width is present. Two layers of surrounding moats encircle the tumulus, and including these, the total length of the kofun is about 60 meters north–south and an estimated 75 meters east–west{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The inner moat is approximately 6 meters wide and 0.8–1.2 meters deep, while the rampart (the embankment between the inner and outer moats) measures about 6 meters in width{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. Numerous wooden artifacts and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been excavated from the inner moat; many of these wooden artifacts, including wooden haniwa, are thought to have been used along with haniwa to encircle the tumulus. The outer moat, which reaches a maximum width of approximately 6 meters and a depth of 0.5 meters on the northern side, is shallow and irregular—suggesting it was excavated for the construction of the rampart{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The burial facility remains unclear due to the leveling. The construction period is estimated to be around the late 5th century of the middle Kofun period{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Many wooden artifacts were detected. Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|Haniwa Horse

Bottom: {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}}}}}} As imperial tombs in close proximity to the Shijō Kofun Group, a hill designated as the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tsukayama) is located 100 meters south of Tumulus No.7 ({{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}})), and approximately 350 meters further to the south-southwest is a hill designated as the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (Misanzai) ({{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. However, the current {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}} are designations from the end of the Edo period, and there has long been a theory that Tsukayama should be identified as the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since both Tsukayama and Misanzai are currently designated as {{ill|Emperor’s tomb|ja|天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, the archaeological details remain unclear{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Tsukayama, having a mound approximately 30 meters in diameter, is considered a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in that case, it would be one of the constituent tumuli of the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Furthermore, Misanzai, in which {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sueki|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} have been confirmed, might originally have been a kofun and thus possibly a constituent tumulus of the group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. According to literature, in the entry for July 23 of the first year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|672|ja|672|12=draft|12=draft}}) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is recorded that Emperor Tenmu offered horses and weapons to the tomb of {{ill|Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja|神日本磐余彦天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} – indicating that a tomb for Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} existed even before the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. In general, since Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} is widely regarded as a progenitor king, it is theorized that during the Tenmu period a need arose to create a tomb for the progenitor king, and there is a theory that the pre-existing Misanzai (or Tsukayama) was repurposed without being leveled; another theory relates the creation of the progenitor king’s tomb to the construction plan of Fujiwara-kyō{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. It is also proposed that the current area of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}} was originally the temple grounds of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} (now a defunct temple). According to this theory, the small hill that existed before the designation of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}} was a square, mound-like structure, and from the place name "塔垣内" in the surrounding area, it has been suggested that this hill originally formed part of the pagoda base of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}.「大窪寺跡」 in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft|12=draft}} 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1981. There is also a theory that {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} itself served as the founding temple (or grave-side temple) for the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=draft|12=draft}}, 『Invitation to the Imperial Tomb Kofun ({{ill|Chikuma Sensho|ja|筑摩選書|12=draft|12=draft}}23)』, {{ill|Chikuma Shobō|ja|筑摩書房|12=draft|12=draft}}, 2011, pp. 259–263. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Unebayacho|ja|畝傍町|12=draft|12=draft}}) – Stores and exhibits the artifacts excavated from the Shijō Kofun Group. == References == {{Footnote {{Coordinates list}} '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群) is a group of ancient tombs ({{ill|Kofun Group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Shijō Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), 14 tumuli have been confirmed; however, all have been destroyed by later generations and exist only as buried tombs with no surviving mounds on the surface. == Overview == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群)}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Suizei Tomb|lt_ja=現・綏靖陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Jimmu Tomb|lt_ja=現・神武陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Center|{{Smaller|(Estimated location within {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}}} {{Image label end}} Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and near the intersection of Shijō Town and the northeast side of {{ill|Unebayama|ja|畝傍山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this kofun group was constructed from the early part of the middle Kofun period to the early part of the late Kofun period (from the first half of the 5th century to the mid-6th century){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. The entire area lies within the bounds of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is said that at the end of the 7th century, with the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, the mounds were leveled and the moats were filled in{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 62), the first tumulus (Tumulus No.1) was discovered during the initial excavation survey; subsequently, due to investigations required for the construction of the {{ill|Yamato Takada Bypass|ja|大和高田バイパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a total of 31 surveys were conducted, resulting in the discovery of 14 tumuli as of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}. Although the burial facilities are unclear due to the leveling of the mounds, many wooden artifacts have been found in excellent condition from the moats – presumably because the filled-in moats retained moisture{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}. In particular, Tumulus No.1 has revealed its entire structure as a square tumulus with a built‐in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) component, with numerous haniwa and wooden artifacts recovered{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In addition to the tumuli, settlement traces from the Kofun period were also discovered, and together they are collectively referred to as the "Shijō Iseki"{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Near the kofun group lie the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) and the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama), suggesting a possible relationship between these early imperial tombs and the kofun group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. == List == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Shijō Kofun Group (Bold indicates principal tumulus) {{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} ! order of Tumulus No.7 → No.2 → No.1, and that the peak period of the kofun group was from the latter half to the end of the middle period{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|Tumulus No.6 – House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Explanatory sign 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Wooden artifact in the shape of a bird
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|Tumulus No.14 (Excavation survey in 2019) == Tumulus No.1 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus No.1}}{{small|Photo taken during the 2017 excavation survey.}}]] '''Tumulus No.1''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was the first tumulus discovered within the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}} ({{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}), and excavation surveys by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others have been conducted since {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the mound was destroyed and its fill lost during the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, it originally measured approximately 32 meters north–south and 40 meters east–west (including the built-in structure){{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. On the western side of the mound, a trapezoidal built-in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) measuring 9 meters in length and 14 meters in width is present. Two layers of surrounding moats encircle the tumulus, and including these, the total length of the kofun is about 60 meters north–south and an estimated 75 meters east–west{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The inner moat is approximately 6 meters wide and 0.8–1.2 meters deep, while the rampart (the embankment between the inner and outer moats) measures about 6 meters in width{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. Numerous wooden artifacts and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been excavated from the inner moat; many of these wooden artifacts, including wooden haniwa, are thought to have been used along with haniwa to encircle the tumulus. The outer moat, which reaches a maximum width of approximately 6 meters and a depth of 0.5 meters on the northern side, is shallow and irregular—suggesting it was excavated for the construction of the rampart{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The burial facility remains unclear due to the leveling. The construction period is estimated to be around the late 5th century of the middle Kofun period{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Many wooden artifacts were detected. Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|Haniwa Horse
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条1号墳出土 鹿形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa Deer (Reclining Deer)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Relationship with the Tombs of Emperor Jimmu and Emperor Suizei == {{Double image stack|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|Top: {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Bottom: {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}}}} As imperial tombs in close proximity to the Shijō Kofun Group, a hill designated as the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama) is located 100 meters south of Tumulus No.7 ({{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}})), and approximately 350 meters further to the south-southwest is a hill designated as the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) ({{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. However, the current {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are designations from the end of the Edo period, and there has long been a theory that Tsukayama should be identified as the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since both Tsukayama and Misanzai are currently designated as {{ill|Emperor’s tomb|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the archaeological details remain unclear{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Tsukayama, having a mound approximately 30 meters in diameter, is considered a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in that case, it would be one of the constituent tumuli of the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Furthermore, Misanzai, in which {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sueki|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been confirmed, might originally have been a kofun and thus possibly a constituent tumulus of the group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. According to literature, in the entry for July 23 of the first year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|672|ja|672|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded that Emperor Tenmu offered horses and weapons to the tomb of {{ill|Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja|神日本磐余彦天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – indicating that a tomb for Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} existed even before the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. In general, since Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is widely regarded as a progenitor king, it is theorized that during the Tenmu period a need arose to create a tomb for the progenitor king, and there is a theory that the pre-existing Misanzai (or Tsukayama) was repurposed without being leveled; another theory relates the creation of the progenitor king’s tomb to the construction plan of Fujiwara-kyō{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. It is also proposed that the current area of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was originally the temple grounds of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now a defunct temple). According to this theory, the small hill that existed before the designation of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a square, mound-like structure, and from the place name "塔垣内" in the surrounding area, it has been suggested that this hill originally formed part of the pagoda base of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.「大窪寺跡」 in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1981. There is also a theory that {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} itself served as the founding temple (or grave-side temple) for the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 『Invitation to the Imperial Tomb Kofun ({{ill|Chikuma Sensho|ja|筑摩選書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}23)』, {{ill|Chikuma Shobō|ja|筑摩書房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 2011, pp. 259–263. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in revision.text:
{{座標一覧}} '''四条古墳群'''（しじょうこふんぐん）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]四条町にある[[古墳群]]。 [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）時点で14基が確認されているが、いずれも後世に破壊され、地上に墳丘としては存在しない埋没古墳である。 == 概要 == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''四条古墳群'''}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● [[綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|'''現・綏靖陵''']]}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● [[神武天皇#陵・霊廟|'''現・神武陵''']]}} {{Center|{{Smaller|[[藤原京]]（推定）における位置}}}} {{Image label end}} [[奈良盆地]]南部、[[畝傍山]]北東の四条町交差点付近に位置し、[[古墳時代]]中期前半から後期前半（[[5世紀]]前半から[[6世紀]]中頃）にかけて築造された[[古墳群]]である{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。一帯は[[藤原京]]の域内にあたり、[[7世紀]]末の藤原京造営に伴い、墳丘が削平されて周濠も埋め立てられたとされる{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 [[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）の第1次発掘調査において初めて古墳が見つかり（1号墳）、その後[[大和高田バイパス]]の建設に伴う調査の必要もあって計31次の調査が行われた結果、[[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）時点で14基の古墳が発見されている{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}。墳丘削平のため古墳の埋葬施設は明らかでないが、周濠からは濠の埋め立てで水分を含んだことによって多くの木製品が良好な保存状態で見つかっている{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。特に1号墳では、[[造出]]付き[[方墳]]としての全容が明らかとなり、多数の[[埴輪]]・木製品が確認されている{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。調査では、古墳のほかに古墳時代の集落跡も発見され、それらは古墳と併せて「四条遺跡」と総称される{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 古墳群付近には初代[[神武天皇]]陵（ミサンザイ）・第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵（塚山）があり、これら初期天皇陵と古墳群との関連性も指摘される{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 == 一覧 == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+四条古墳群の一覧（太字は主墳）{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造順 !築造時期 !備考 |- |'''1号墳'''||[[造出]]付き[[方墳]]||墳丘長38m||4||中期末-後期初頭||多様な埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |'''2号墳'''||[[帆立貝形古墳|帆立貝式古墳]]||墳丘長42m||3||中期後半||多様な埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |3号墳||方墳||一辺18m||5||後期前半||埴輪の出土 |- |4号墳||[[円墳]]||直径22.5m||1||中期前半|| |- |5号墳||方墳||一辺15-16m||4||中期末-後期初頭||木製品の出土 |- |6号墳||円墳||直径18m||5||後期前半||埴輪の出土 |- |'''7号墳'''||円墳または[[前方後円墳]]||直径65m||2||中期後半||多様な埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |8号墳||円墳||直径13.5m||3||中期後半|| |- |9号墳||円墳||直径26m||4||中期末-後期初頭||埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |10号墳||方墳||一辺10.5-12m||4||中期末-後期初頭||埴輪の出土 |- |11号墳|||| || || || |- |12号墳||方墳|| ||4||中期末-後期初頭||埴輪の出土 |- |13号墳|| || || || || |- |14号墳||造出付き円墳||直径25m|| ||後期初頭||埴輪の出土 |} 主墳は7号墳→2号墳→1号墳の順に営まれたと見られ、中期後半から中期末にかけてが古墳群の最盛期と見られる{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|6号墳 家形埴輪
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|9号墳 説明板 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|9号墳 鳥形木製品
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|14号墳（2019年度発掘調査時） == 四条1号墳 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|四条1号墳}}{{small|2017年度発掘調査時。}}]] '''四条1号墳'''は、[[方墳]]。四条古墳群のうち初めて見つかった古墳で{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}（{{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}）、1987年（昭和62年）以後に[[橿原考古学研究所]]などによる発掘調査が実施されている。 墳丘は藤原京造成時に破壊されて盛土が失われているが、元来は方形で、南北約32メートル・東西約40メートル（造出含む）を測る{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。墳丘西側には長さ9メートル・幅14メートルの台形の[[造出]]が付される。墳丘周囲には2重の周濠が巡らされ、周濠を含めた古墳全長は南北約60メートル・東西推定75メートルを測る{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。内濠は幅6メートル・深さ約0.8-1.2メートル、周堤（内濠・外濠間の堤）は幅約6メートルを測る{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。内濠からは多数の木製品及び[[埴輪]]が出土しており、特に木製品は木製埴輪も含んで良好な状態で大量に出土している。これらの木製品の多くは、埴輪とともに古墳の周囲を囲うために用いられたとされる。外濠幅は北辺で最大幅約6メートル・深さ0.5メートルを測るが、浅く不整形であるため、周堤築造の目的のための掘削と推測される{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。埋葬施設は削平のため明らかでない。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末頃と推定される{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|多数の木製品を検出。奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|埴輪 馬
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} 四条1号墳出土 鹿形埴輪.JPG|埴輪 鹿（見返り鹿）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} == 神武天皇陵・綏靖天皇陵との関係 == {{Double image stack|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|上：[[神武天皇]]陵
下：[[綏靖天皇]]陵}}}} 四条古墳群に近接する[[天皇陵]]として、7号墳の南100メートルの地に第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵に治定される丘陵「塚山」（{{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}}）が、そのさらに南南西約350メートルの地には初代[[神武天皇]]陵に治定される丘陵「ミサンザイ」（{{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}）がある{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。ただし、現在の神武天皇陵・綏靖天皇陵は幕末の治定によるものであり、塚山を神武天皇陵に比定する説も古くから存在する。塚山・ミサンザイとも現在は天皇陵に治定されているため、考古学的詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。塚山は直径約30メートルの墳丘を持つことから[[円墳]]であると見られ、その場合は四条古墳群の構成古墳の1つになる{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。またミサンザイも、[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]等が確認されたことから元々古墳であった可能性があり、四条古墳群の構成古墳であった可能性もある{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 文献上では、『[[日本書紀]]』[[天武天皇]]元年（[[672年]]）7月23日条において、天武天皇が「神日本磐余彦天皇之陵」に馬や兵器を奉ったと見え、藤原京造営以前から神武天皇陵が存在したことが知られる{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}。一般に神武天皇は始祖王として創造された人物とする説が有力的であるため、天武朝頃に始祖王陵を創造する必要が生じ、当地に元からあったミサンザイ（または塚山）が削平されずに転用されたと推測する説のほか{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}、その始祖王陵創造と藤原京建設計画に関連性をみる説もある{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 なお、現在の神武天皇陵の陵域に関して、元々は[[大窪寺跡|大窪寺]]（現在は廃寺）の寺域であったとする説もある。この説では、神武天皇陵の治定前にあった小丘が方形の土壇状であったということや、周辺の「塔垣内」の字名から、その小丘が本来は大窪寺の塔基壇の一部であった可能性が指摘される「大窪寺跡」 『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年。。また大窪寺自体を、神武天皇陵の陵寺や墓辺寺としての開基とする説もある[[森浩一]] 『天皇陵古墳への招待（筑摩選書23）』 筑摩書房、2011年、pp. 259-263。。 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 四条古墳群の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 橿原考古学研究所発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/event/atrium/2010/shijyo-site.pdf 「四条遺跡 -大和高田バイパス建設関連の発掘調査とその成果-」]}}（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] アトリウム展示資料）、2010年。|ref={{Harvid|橿考研|2010}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2017/11/2017_shijyo36.pdf 「四条遺跡第36次・藤原京右京四条六坊 発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、2017年）。|ref={{Harvid|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}}} - リンクは奈良県立橿原考古学研究所。 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/3c01c323ac8ee1d2e85e933c9f35443e.pdf 「藤原京右京五条六坊・四条遺跡（第38次調査） 現地説明会資料」]}}（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、2019年）。|ref={{Harvid|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}}} - リンクは奈良県立橿原考古学研究所。 * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=四条古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=石野博信|authorlink=石野博信|year=1993|chapter=四条古墳|title=日本史大事典 3|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=978-4582131031|ref={{Harvid|四条古墳（日本史）|1993}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=犬木努|year=|chapter=四条古墳|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|四条古墳（国史）}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=[[大和書房]]|isbn=4479840656|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=泉武 「四条古墳」|ref={{Harvid|四条古墳（古代史）|2006}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=[[和田萃]] 「神武天皇」|ref={{Harvid|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}}}。 == 関連項目 == * [[神武天皇]] * [[綏靖天皇]] * [[大窪寺跡]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shijo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/shijyo_kofungun.html 四条古墳群]、[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/shijou_ootanaka.html 四条遺跡【大田中地区】] - 橿原市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:ししようこふんくん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:藤原京]]

revision.text:
{{座標一覧}} '''四条古墳群'''（しじょうこふんぐん）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]四条町にある[[古墳群]]。 [[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）時点で14基が確認されているが、いずれも後世に破壊され、地上に墳丘としては存在しない埋没古墳である。 == 概要 == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''四条古墳群'''}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● [[綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|'''現・綏靖陵''']]}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● [[神武天皇#陵・霊廟|'''現・神武陵''']]}} {{Center|{{Smaller|[[藤原京]]（推定）における位置}}}} {{Image label end}} [[奈良盆地]]南部、[[畝傍山]]北東の四条町交差点付近に位置し、[[古墳時代]]中期前半から後期前半（[[5世紀]]前半から[[6世紀]]中頃）にかけて築造された[[古墳群]]である{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。一帯は[[藤原京]]の域内にあたり、[[7世紀]]末の藤原京造営に伴い、墳丘が削平されて周濠も埋め立てられたとされる{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 [[1987年]]（[[昭和]]62年）の第1次発掘調査において初めて古墳が見つかり（1号墳）、その後[[大和高田バイパス]]の建設に伴う調査の必要もあって計31次の調査が行われた結果、[[2019年]]（[[令和]]元年）時点で14基の古墳が発見されている{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}。墳丘削平のため古墳の埋葬施設は明らかでないが、周濠からは濠の埋め立てで水分を含んだことによって多くの木製品が良好な保存状態で見つかっている{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。特に1号墳では、[[造出]]付き[[方墳]]としての全容が明らかとなり、多数の[[埴輪]]・木製品が確認されている{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。調査では、古墳のほかに古墳時代の集落跡も発見され、それらは古墳と併せて「四条遺跡」と総称される{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 古墳群付近には初代[[神武天皇]]陵（ミサンザイ）・第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵（塚山）があり、これら初期天皇陵と古墳群との関連性も指摘される{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 == 一覧 == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+四条古墳群の一覧（太字は主墳）{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !築造順 !築造時期 !備考 |- |'''1号墳'''||[[造出]]付き[[方墳]]||墳丘長38m||4||中期末-後期初頭||多様な埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |'''2号墳'''||[[帆立貝形古墳|帆立貝式古墳]]||墳丘長42m||3||中期後半||多様な埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |3号墳||方墳||一辺18m||5||後期前半||埴輪の出土 |- |4号墳||[[円墳]]||直径22.5m||1||中期前半|| |- |5号墳||方墳||一辺15-16m||4||中期末-後期初頭||木製品の出土 |- |6号墳||円墳||直径18m||5||後期前半||埴輪の出土 |- |'''7号墳'''||円墳または[[前方後円墳]]||直径65m||2||中期後半||多様な埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |8号墳||円墳||直径13.5m||3||中期後半|| |- |9号墳||円墳||直径26m||4||中期末-後期初頭||埴輪・木製品の出土 |- |10号墳||方墳||一辺10.5-12m||4||中期末-後期初頭||埴輪の出土 |- |11号墳|||| || || || |- |12号墳||方墳|| ||4||中期末-後期初頭||埴輪の出土 |- |13号墳|| || || || || |- |14号墳||造出付き円墳||直径25m|| ||後期初頭||埴輪の出土 |} 主墳は7号墳→2号墳→1号墳の順に営まれたと見られ、中期後半から中期末にかけてが古墳群の最盛期と見られる{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|6号墳 家形埴輪
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|9号墳 説明板 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|9号墳 鳥形木製品
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|14号墳（2019年度発掘調査時） == 四条1号墳 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|四条1号墳}}{{small|2017年度発掘調査時。}}]] '''四条1号墳'''は、[[方墳]]。四条古墳群のうち初めて見つかった古墳で{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}（{{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}）、1987年（昭和62年）以後に[[橿原考古学研究所]]などによる発掘調査が実施されている。 墳丘は藤原京造成時に破壊されて盛土が失われているが、元来は方形で、南北約32メートル・東西約40メートル（造出含む）を測る{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。墳丘西側には長さ9メートル・幅14メートルの台形の[[造出]]が付される。墳丘周囲には2重の周濠が巡らされ、周濠を含めた古墳全長は南北約60メートル・東西推定75メートルを測る{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。内濠は幅6メートル・深さ約0.8-1.2メートル、周堤（内濠・外濠間の堤）は幅約6メートルを測る{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。内濠からは多数の木製品及び[[埴輪]]が出土しており、特に木製品は木製埴輪も含んで良好な状態で大量に出土している。これらの木製品の多くは、埴輪とともに古墳の周囲を囲うために用いられたとされる。外濠幅は北辺で最大幅約6メートル・深さ0.5メートルを測るが、浅く不整形であるため、周堤築造の目的のための掘削と推測される{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。埋葬施設は削平のため明らかでない。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]末頃と推定される{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}。 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|多数の木製品を検出。奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|埴輪 馬
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} 四条1号墳出土 鹿形埴輪.JPG|埴輪 鹿（見返り鹿）
{{small|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館展示。}} == 神武天皇陵・綏靖天皇陵との関係 == {{Double image stack|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|上：[[神武天皇]]陵
下：[[綏靖天皇]]陵}}}} 四条古墳群に近接する[[天皇陵]]として、7号墳の南100メートルの地に第2代[[綏靖天皇]]陵に治定される丘陵「塚山」（{{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}}）が、そのさらに南南西約350メートルの地には初代[[神武天皇]]陵に治定される丘陵「ミサンザイ」（{{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}）がある{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。ただし、現在の神武天皇陵・綏靖天皇陵は幕末の治定によるものであり、塚山を神武天皇陵に比定する説も古くから存在する。塚山・ミサンザイとも現在は天皇陵に治定されているため、考古学的詳細は明らかでない{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。塚山は直径約30メートルの墳丘を持つことから[[円墳]]であると見られ、その場合は四条古墳群の構成古墳の1つになる{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。またミサンザイも、[[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]等が確認されたことから元々古墳であった可能性があり、四条古墳群の構成古墳であった可能性もある{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 文献上では、『[[日本書紀]]』[[天武天皇]]元年（[[672年]]）7月23日条において、天武天皇が「神日本磐余彦天皇之陵」に馬や兵器を奉ったと見え、藤原京造営以前から神武天皇陵が存在したことが知られる{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}。一般に神武天皇は始祖王として創造された人物とする説が有力的であるため、天武朝頃に始祖王陵を創造する必要が生じ、当地に元からあったミサンザイ（または塚山）が削平されずに転用されたと推測する説のほか{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}、その始祖王陵創造と藤原京建設計画に関連性をみる説もある{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}。 なお、現在の神武天皇陵の陵域に関して、元々は[[大窪寺跡|大窪寺]]（現在は廃寺）の寺域であったとする説もある。この説では、神武天皇陵の治定前にあった小丘が方形の土壇状であったということや、周辺の「塔垣内」の字名から、その小丘が本来は大窪寺の塔基壇の一部であった可能性が指摘される「大窪寺跡」 『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 平凡社、1981年。。また大窪寺自体を、神武天皇陵の陵寺や墓辺寺としての開基とする説もある[[森浩一]] 『天皇陵古墳への招待（筑摩選書23）』 筑摩書房、2011年、pp. 259-263。。 == 関連施設 == * [[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館（橿原市畝傍町） - 四条古墳群の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 橿原考古学研究所発行 ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/event/atrium/2010/shijyo-site.pdf 「四条遺跡 -大和高田バイパス建設関連の発掘調査とその成果-」]}}（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]] アトリウム展示資料）、2010年。|ref={{Harvid|橿考研|2010}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/under_construction/wp-content/uploads/2017/11/2017_shijyo36.pdf 「四条遺跡第36次・藤原京右京四条六坊 発掘調査 現地説明会資料」]}}（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、2017年）。|ref={{Harvid|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}}} - 橿原市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:ししようこふんくん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:橿原市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:藤原京]]

{{Coordinates list}} '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群) is a group of ancient tombs ({{ill|Kofun Group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Shijō Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), 14 tumuli have been confirmed; however, all have been destroyed by later generations and exist only as buried tombs with no surviving mounds on the surface. == Overview == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群)}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Suizei Tomb|lt_ja=現・綏靖陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Jimmu Tomb|lt_ja=現・神武陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Center|{{Smaller|(Estimated location within {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}}} {{Image label end}} Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and near the intersection of Shijō Town and the northeast side of {{ill|Unebayama|ja|畝傍山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this kofun group was constructed from the early part of the middle Kofun period to the early part of the late Kofun period (from the first half of the 5th century to the mid-6th century){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. The entire area lies within the bounds of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is said that at the end of the 7th century, with the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, the mounds were leveled and the moats were filled in{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 62), the first tumulus (Tumulus No.1) was discovered during the initial excavation survey; subsequently, due to investigations required for the construction of the {{ill|Yamato Takada Bypass|ja|大和高田バイパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a total of 31 surveys were conducted, resulting in the discovery of 14 tumuli as of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}. Although the burial facilities are unclear due to the leveling of the mounds, many wooden artifacts have been found in excellent condition from the moats – presumably because the filled-in moats retained moisture{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}. In particular, Tumulus No.1 has revealed its entire structure as a square tumulus with a built‐in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) component, with numerous haniwa and wooden artifacts recovered{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In addition to the tumuli, settlement traces from the Kofun period were also discovered, and together they are collectively referred to as the "Shijō Iseki"{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Near the kofun group lie the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) and the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama), suggesting a possible relationship between these early imperial tombs and the kofun group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. == List == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Shijō Kofun Group (Bold indicates principal tumulus) {{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} ! order of Tumulus No.7 → No.2 → No.1, and that the peak period of the kofun group was from the latter half to the end of the middle period{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|Tumulus No.6 – House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Explanatory sign 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Wooden artifact in the shape of a bird
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|Tumulus No.14 (Excavation survey in 2019) == Tumulus No.1 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus No.1}}{{small|Photo taken during the 2017 excavation survey.}}]] '''Tumulus No.1''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was the first tumulus discovered within the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}} ({{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}), and excavation surveys by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others have been conducted since {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the mound was destroyed and its fill lost during the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, it originally measured approximately 32 meters north–south and 40 meters east–west (including the built-in structure){{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. On the western side of the mound, a trapezoidal built-in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) measuring 9 meters in length and 14 meters in width is present. Two layers of surrounding moats encircle the tumulus, and including these, the total length of the kofun is about 60 meters north–south and an estimated 75 meters east–west{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The inner moat is approximately 6 meters wide and 0.8–1.2 meters deep, while the rampart (the embankment between the inner and outer moats) measures about 6 meters in width{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. Numerous wooden artifacts and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been excavated from the inner moat; many of these wooden artifacts, including wooden haniwa, are thought to have been used along with haniwa to encircle the tumulus. The outer moat, which reaches a maximum width of approximately 6 meters and a depth of 0.5 meters on the northern side, is shallow and irregular—suggesting it was excavated for the construction of the rampart{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The burial facility remains unclear due to the leveling. The construction period is estimated to be around the late 5th century of the middle Kofun period{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Many wooden artifacts were detected. Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|Haniwa Horse
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条1号墳出土 鹿形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa Deer (Reclining Deer)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Relationship with the Tombs of Emperor Jimmu and Emperor Suizei == {{Double image stack|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|Top: {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Bottom: {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}}}} As imperial tombs in close proximity to the Shijō Kofun Group, a hill designated as the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama) is located 100 meters south of Tumulus No.7 ({{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}})), and approximately 350 meters further to the south-southwest is a hill designated as the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) ({{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. However, the current {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are designations from the end of the Edo period, and there has long been a theory that Tsukayama should be identified as the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since both Tsukayama and Misanzai are currently designated as {{ill|Emperor’s tomb|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the archaeological details remain unclear{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Tsukayama, having a mound approximately 30 meters in diameter, is considered a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in that case, it would be one of the constituent tumuli of the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Furthermore, Misanzai, in which {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sueki|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been confirmed, might originally have been a kofun and thus possibly a constituent tumulus of the group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. According to literature, in the entry for July 23 of the first year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|672|ja|672|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded that Emperor Tenmu offered horses and weapons to the tomb of {{ill|Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja|神日本磐余彦天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – indicating that a tomb for Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} existed even before the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. In general, since Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is widely regarded as a progenitor king, it is theorized that during the Tenmu period a need arose to create a tomb for the progenitor king, and there is a theory that the pre-existing Misanzai (or Tsukayama) was repurposed without being leveled; another theory relates the creation of the progenitor king’s tomb to the construction plan of Fujiwara-kyō{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. It is also proposed that the current area of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was originally the temple grounds of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now a defunct temple). According to this theory, the small hill that existed before the designation of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a square, mound-like structure, and from the place name "塔垣内" in the surrounding area, it has been suggested that this hill originally formed part of the pagoda base of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.「大窪寺跡」 in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1981. There is also a theory that {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} itself served as the founding temple (or grave-side temple) for the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 『Invitation to the Imperial Tomb Kofun ({{ill|Chikuma Sensho|ja|筑摩選書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}23)』, {{ill|Chikuma Shobō|ja|筑摩書房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 2011, pp. 259–263. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Unebayacho|ja|畝傍町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – Stores and exhibits the artifacts excavated from the Shijō Kofun Group. == References == {{Footnote history}}

{{Coordinates list}} '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群) is a group of ancient tombs ({{ill|Kofun Group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Shijō Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), 14 tumuli have been confirmed; however, all have been destroyed by later generations and exist only as buried tombs with no surviving mounds on the surface. == Overview == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群)}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Suizei Tomb|lt_ja=現・綏靖陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Jimmu Tomb|lt_ja=現・神武陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Center|{{Smaller|(Estimated location within {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}}} {{Image label end}} Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and near the intersection of Shijō Town and the northeast side of {{ill|Unebayama|ja|畝傍山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this kofun group was constructed from the early part of the middle Kofun period to the early part of the late Kofun period (from the first half of the 5th century to the mid-6th century){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. The entire area lies within the bounds of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is said that at the end of the 7th century, with the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, the mounds were leveled and the moats were filled in{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 62), the first tumulus (Tumulus No.1) was discovered during the initial excavation survey; subsequently, due to investigations required for the construction of the {{ill|Yamato Takada Bypass|ja|大和高田バイパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a total of 31 surveys were conducted, resulting in the discovery of 14 tumuli as of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}. Although the burial facilities are unclear due to the leveling of the mounds, many wooden artifacts have been found in excellent condition from the moats – presumably because the filled-in moats retained moisture{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}. In particular, Tumulus No.1 has revealed its entire structure as a square tumulus with a built‐in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) component, with numerous haniwa and wooden artifacts recovered{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In addition to the tumuli, settlement traces from the Kofun period were also discovered, and together they are collectively referred to as the "Shijō Iseki"{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Near the kofun group lie the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) and the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama), suggesting a possible relationship between these early imperial tombs and the kofun group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. == List == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Shijō Kofun Group (Bold indicates principal tumulus) {{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} ! {{small|Photo taken during an exhibition by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Explanatory sign 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Wooden artifact in the shape of a bird
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|Tumulus No.14 (Excavation survey in 2019) == Tumulus No.1 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus No.1}}{{small|Photo taken during the 2017 excavation survey.}}]] '''Tumulus No.1''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was the first tumulus discovered within the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}} ({{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}), and excavation surveys by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others have been conducted since {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the mound was destroyed and its fill lost during the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, it originally measured approximately 32 meters north–south and 40 meters east–west (including the built-in structure){{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. On the western side of the mound, a trapezoidal built-in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) measuring 9 meters in length and 14 meters in width is present. Two layers of surrounding moats encircle the tumulus, and including these, the total length of the kofun is about 60 meters north–south and an estimated 75 meters east–west{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The inner moat is approximately 6 meters wide and 0.8–1.2 meters deep, while the rampart (the embankment between the inner and outer moats) measures about 6 meters in width{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. Numerous wooden artifacts and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been excavated from the inner moat; many of these wooden artifacts, including wooden haniwa, are thought to have been used along with haniwa to encircle the tumulus. The outer moat, which reaches a maximum width of approximately 6 meters and a depth of 0.5 meters on the northern side, is shallow and irregular—suggesting it was excavated for the construction of the rampart{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The burial facility remains unclear due to the leveling. The construction period is estimated to be around the late 5th century of the middle Kofun period{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Many wooden artifacts were detected. Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|Haniwa Horse
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条1号墳出土 鹿形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa Deer (Reclining Deer)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Relationship with the Tombs of Emperor Jimmu and Emperor Suizei == {{Double image stack|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|Top: {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Bottom: {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}}}} As imperial tombs in close proximity to the Shijō Kofun Group, a hill designated as the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama) is located 100 meters south of Tumulus No.7 ({{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}})), and approximately 350 meters further to the south-southwest is a hill designated as the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) ({{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. However, the current {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are designations from the end of the Edo period, and there has long been a theory that Tsukayama should be identified as the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since both Tsukayama and Misanzai are currently designated as {{ill|Emperor’s tomb|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the archaeological details remain unclear{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Tsukayama, having a mound approximately 30 meters in diameter, is considered a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in that case, it would be one of the constituent tumuli of the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Furthermore, Misanzai, in which {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sueki|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been confirmed, might originally have been a kofun and thus possibly a constituent tumulus of the group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. According to literature, in the entry for July 23 of the first year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|672|ja|672|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded that Emperor Tenmu offered horses and weapons to the tomb of {{ill|Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja|神日本磐余彦天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – indicating that a tomb for Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} existed even before the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. In general, since Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is widely regarded as a progenitor king, it is theorized that during the Tenmu period a need arose to create a tomb for the progenitor king, and there is a theory that the pre-existing Misanzai (or Tsukayama) was repurposed without being leveled; another theory relates the creation of the progenitor king’s tomb to the construction plan of Fujiwara-kyō{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. It is also proposed that the current area of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was originally the temple grounds of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now a defunct temple). According to this theory, the small hill that existed before the designation of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a square, mound-like structure, and from the place name "塔垣内" in the surrounding area, it has been suggested that this hill originally formed part of the pagoda base of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.「大窪寺跡」 in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1981. There is also a theory that {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} itself served as the founding temple (or grave-side temple) for the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 『Invitation to the Imperial Tomb Kofun ({{ill|Chikuma Sensho|ja|筑摩選書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}23)』, {{ill|Chikuma Shobō|ja|筑摩書房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 2011, pp. 259–263. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Unebayacho|ja|畝傍町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – Stores and exhibits the artifacts excavated from the Shijō Kofun Group. == References == {{Footnote [[sn:Shijō Kofun Group]] [[en:Shijō Kofun Group]] [[ja:四条古墳群]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shijō Kofun Group}} {{translated page|ja|四条古墳群|version=100330941|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Coordinates list}} '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群) is a group of ancient tombs ({{ill|Kofun Group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Shijō Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), 14 tumuli have been confirmed; however, all have been destroyed by later generations and exist only as buried tombs with no surviving mounds on the surface. == Overview == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群)}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Suizei Tomb|lt_ja=現・綏靖陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Jimmu Tomb|lt_ja=現・神武陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Center|{{Smaller|(Estimated location within {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}}} {{Image label end}} Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and near the intersection of Shijō Town and the northeast side of {{ill|Unebayama|ja|畝傍山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this kofun group was constructed from the early part of the middle Kofun period to the early part of the late Kofun period (from the first half of the 5th century to the mid-6th century){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. The entire area lies within the bounds of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is said that at the end of the 7th century, with the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, the mounds were leveled and the moats were filled in{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 62), the first tumulus (Tumulus No.1) was discovered during the initial excavation survey; subsequently, due to investigations required for the construction of the {{ill|Yamato Takada Bypass|ja|大和高田バイパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a total of 31 surveys were conducted, resulting in the discovery of 14 tumuli as of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}. Although the burial facilities are unclear due to the leveling of the mounds, many wooden artifacts have been found in excellent condition from the moats – presumably because the filled-in moats retained moisture{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}. In particular, Tumulus No.1 has revealed its entire structure as a square tumulus with a built‐in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) component, with numerous haniwa and wooden artifacts recovered{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In addition to the tumuli, settlement traces from the Kofun period were also discovered, and together they are collectively referred to as the "Shijō Iseki"{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Near the kofun group lie the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) and the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama), suggesting a possible relationship between these early imperial tombs and the kofun group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. == List == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Shijō Kofun Group (Bold indicates principal tumulus) {{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} ! order of Tumulus No.7 → No.2 → No.1, and that the peak period of the kofun group was from the latter half to the end of the middle period{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|Tumulus No.6 – House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Explanatory sign 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Wooden artifact in the shape of a bird
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|Tumulus No.14 (Excavation survey in 2019) == Tumulus No.1 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus No.1}}{{small|Photo taken during the 2017 excavation survey.}}]] '''Tumulus No.1''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was the first tumulus discovered within the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}} ({{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}), and excavation surveys by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others have been conducted since {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the mound was destroyed and its fill lost during the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, it originally measured approximately 32 meters north–south and 40 meters east–west (including the built-in structure){{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. On the western side of the mound, a trapezoidal built-in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) measuring 9 meters in length and 14 meters in width is present. Two layers of surrounding moats encircle the tumulus, and including these, the total length of the kofun is about 60 meters north–south and an estimated 75 meters east–west{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The inner moat is approximately 6 meters wide and 0.8–1.2 meters deep, while the rampart (the embankment between the inner and outer moats) measures about 6 meters in width{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. Numerous wooden artifacts and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been excavated from the inner moat; many of these wooden artifacts, including wooden haniwa, are thought to have been used along with haniwa to encircle the tumulus. The outer moat, which reaches a maximum width of approximately 6 meters and a depth of 0.5 meters on the northern side, is shallow and irregular—suggesting it was excavated for the construction of the rampart{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The burial facility remains unclear due to the leveling. The construction period is estimated to be around the late 5th century of the middle Kofun period{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Many wooden artifacts were detected. Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|Haniwa Horse

{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Relationship with the Tombs of Emperor Jimmu and Emperor Suizei == {{Double image stack|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|Top: {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Bottom: {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}}}} As imperial tombs in close proximity to the Shijō Kofun Group, a hill designated as the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama) is located 100 meters south of Tumulus No.7 ({{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}})), and approximately 350 meters further to the south-southwest is a hill designated as the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) ({{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. However, the current {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are designations from the end of the Edo period, and there has long been a theory that Tsukayama should be identified as the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since both Tsukayama and Misanzai are currently designated as {{ill|Emperor’s tomb|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the archaeological details remain unclear{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Tsukayama, having a mound approximately 30 meters in diameter, is considered a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in that case, it would be one of the constituent tumuli of the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Furthermore, Misanzai, in which {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sueki|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been confirmed, might originally have been a kofun and thus possibly a constituent tumulus of the group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. According to literature, in the entry for July 23 of the first year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|672|ja|672|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded that Emperor Tenmu offered horses and weapons to the tomb of {{ill|Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja|神日本磐余彦天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – indicating that a tomb for Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} existed even before the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. In general, since Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is widely regarded as a progenitor king, it is theorized that during the Tenmu period a need arose to create a tomb for the progenitor king, and there is a theory that the pre-existing Misanzai (or Tsukayama) was repurposed without being leveled; another theory relates the creation of the progenitor king’s tomb to the construction plan of Fujiwara-kyō{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. It is also proposed that the current area of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was originally the temple grounds of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now a defunct temple). According to this theory, the small hill that existed before the designation of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a square, mound-like structure, and from the place name "塔垣内" in the surrounding area, it has been suggested that this hill originally formed part of the pagoda base of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.「大窪寺跡」 in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1981. There is also a theory that {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} itself served as the founding temple (or grave-side temple) for the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 『Invitation to the Imperial Tomb Kofun ({{ill|Chikuma Sensho|ja|筑摩選書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}23)』, {{ill|Chikuma Shobō|ja|筑摩書房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 2011, pp. 259–263. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Unebayacho|ja|畝傍町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) – Stores and exhibits the artifacts excavated from the Shijō Kofun Group. == References == {{Footnote {{Coordinates list}} '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群) is a group of ancient tombs ({{ill|Kofun Group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Shijō Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kashihara City|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. As of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), 14 tumuli have been confirmed; however, all have been destroyed by later generations and exist only as buried tombs with no surviving mounds on the surface. == Overview == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群)}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Suizei Tomb|lt_ja=現・綏靖陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Jimmu Tomb|lt_ja=現・神武陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} {{Center|{{Smaller|(Estimated location within {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}}} {{Image label end}} Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and near the intersection of Shijō Town and the northeast side of {{ill|Unebayama|ja|畝傍山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this kofun group was constructed from the early part of the middle Kofun period to the early part of the late Kofun period (from the first half of the 5th century to the mid-6th century){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. The entire area lies within the bounds of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and it is said that at the end of the 7th century, with the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, the mounds were leveled and the moats were filled in{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 62), the first tumulus (Tumulus No.1) was discovered during the initial excavation survey; subsequently, due to investigations required for the construction of the {{ill|Yamato Takada Bypass|ja|大和高田バイパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a total of 31 surveys were conducted, resulting in the discovery of 14 tumuli as of {{ill|2019|ja|2019|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}. Although the burial facilities are unclear due to the leveling of the mounds, many wooden artifacts have been found in excellent condition from the moats – presumably because the filled-in moats retained moisture{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}. In particular, Tumulus No.1 has revealed its entire structure as a square tumulus with a built‐in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) component, with numerous haniwa and wooden artifacts recovered{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In addition to the tumuli, settlement traces from the Kofun period were also discovered, and together they are collectively referred to as the "Shijō Iseki"{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Near the kofun group lie the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) and the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama), suggesting a possible relationship between these early imperial tombs and the kofun group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. == List == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Shijō Kofun Group (Bold indicates principal tumulus) {{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} ! order of Tumulus No.7 → No.2 → No.1, and that the peak period of the kofun group was from the latter half to the end of the middle period{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|Tumulus No.6 – House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Explanatory sign 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Wooden artifact in the shape of a bird
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|Tumulus No.14 (Excavation survey in 2019) == Tumulus No.1 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus No.1}}{{small|Photo taken during the 2017 excavation survey.}}]] '''Tumulus No.1''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It was the first tumulus discovered within the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}} ({{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}), and excavation surveys by the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others have been conducted since {{ill|1987|ja|1987|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the mound was destroyed and its fill lost during the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, it originally measured approximately 32 meters north–south and 40 meters east–west (including the built-in structure){{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. On the western side of the mound, a trapezoidal built-in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) measuring 9 meters in length and 14 meters in width is present. Two layers of surrounding moats encircle the tumulus, and including these, the total length of the kofun is about 60 meters north–south and an estimated 75 meters east–west{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The inner moat is approximately 6 meters wide and 0.8–1.2 meters deep, while the rampart (the embankment between the inner and outer moats) measures about 6 meters in width{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. Numerous wooden artifacts and {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been excavated from the inner moat; many of these wooden artifacts, including wooden haniwa, are thought to have been used along with haniwa to encircle the tumulus. The outer moat, which reaches a maximum width of approximately 6 meters and a depth of 0.5 meters on the northern side, is shallow and irregular—suggesting it was excavated for the construction of the rampart{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The burial facility remains unclear due to the leveling. The construction period is estimated to be around the late 5th century of the middle Kofun period{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Many wooden artifacts were detected. Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|Haniwa Horse
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} 四条1号墳出土 鹿形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa Deer (Reclining Deer)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.}} == Relationship with the Tombs of Emperor Jimmu and Emperor Suizei == {{Double image stack|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|Top: {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Bottom: {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}}}} As imperial tombs in close proximity to the Shijō Kofun Group, a hill designated as the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tsukayama) is located 100 meters south of Tumulus No.7 ({{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}})), and approximately 350 meters further to the south-southwest is a hill designated as the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Misanzai) ({{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. However, the current {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are designations from the end of the Edo period, and there has long been a theory that Tsukayama should be identified as the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since both Tsukayama and Misanzai are currently designated as {{ill|Emperor’s tomb|ja|天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the archaeological details remain unclear{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Tsukayama, having a mound approximately 30 meters in diameter, is considered a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in that case, it would be one of the constituent tumuli of the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Furthermore, Misanzai, in which {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sueki|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} have been confirmed, might originally have been a kofun and thus possibly a constituent tumulus of the group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. According to literature, in the entry for July 23 of the first year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|672|ja|672|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is recorded that Emperor Tenmu offered horses and weapons to the tomb of {{ill|Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja|神日本磐余彦天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – indicating that a tomb for Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} existed even before the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|ja|藤原京|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. In general, since Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is widely regarded as a progenitor king, it is theorized that during the Tenmu period a need arose to create a tomb for the progenitor king, and there is a theory that the pre-existing Misanzai (or Tsukayama) was repurposed without being leveled; another theory relates the creation of the progenitor king’s tomb to the construction plan of Fujiwara-kyō{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. It is also proposed that the current area of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was originally the temple grounds of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (now a defunct temple). According to this theory, the small hill that existed before the designation of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was a square, mound-like structure, and from the place name "塔垣内" in the surrounding area, it has been suggested that this hill originally formed part of the pagoda base of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.「大窪寺跡」 in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1981. There is also a theory that {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} itself served as the founding temple (or grave-side temple) for the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 『Invitation to the Imperial Tomb Kofun ({{ill|Chikuma Sensho|ja|筑摩選書|qq=draft|qq=draft}}23)』, {{ill|Chikuma Shobō|ja|筑摩書房|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 2011, pp. 259–263. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (in
