[[co:commons:Category:Shijo Kofun Group]][[ja:四条古墳群]] [[co:commons:Category:Shijo Kofun Group]][[ja:四条古墳群]] [[ja:四条古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|location={{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.501514|N|135.791397|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Coordinates list}} '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群) is a group of ancient tombs ({{ill|Kofun Group|ja|古墳群|1=Kofun cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|qq=}}) located in Shijō Town, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara City|1=Kashihara, Nara}}. As of {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}}), 14 tumuli have been confirmed; however, all have been destroyed by later generations and exist only as buried tombs with no surviving mounds on the surface. == Overview == {{Image label begin|image=Fujiwarakyo2.gif|width=350|float=right}} {{Image label|x=0.28 |y=0.365 |scale=350|text={{color|#FC4E6B|● '''Shijō Kofun Group''' (四条古墳群)}}}} {{Image label|x=0.27 |y=0.405 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Suizei Tomb|lt_ja=現・綏靖陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suizei Tomb|1=Emperor Suizei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q313321}}}} {{Image label|x=0.25 |y=0.45 |scale=350|text=● {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇#陵・霊廟|lt=Current Jimmu Tomb|lt_ja=現・神武陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu Tomb|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q200188}}}} {{Center|{{Smaller|(Estimated location within {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō|1=Fujiwara-kyō}})}}}} {{Image label end}} Located in the southern part of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}} and near the intersection of Shijō Town and the northeast side of {{ill|Mount Unebi|en|Mount Unebi|WD=Q17606448|lt=Unebayama|1=Mount Unebi}}, this kofun group was constructed from the early part of the middle Kofun period to the early part of the late Kofun period (from the first half of the 5th century to the mid-6th century){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. The entire area lies within the bounds of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō|1=Fujiwara-kyō}}, and it is said that at the end of the 7th century, with the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, the mounds were leveled and the moats were filled in{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}} (Showa 62), the first tumulus (Tumulus No.1) was discovered during the initial excavation survey; subsequently, due to investigations required for the construction of the {{ill|Yamato Takada Bypass|ja|大和高田バイパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takada Bypass|1=Yamatotakada bypass|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433610}}, a total of 31 surveys were conducted, resulting in the discovery of 14 tumuli as of {{ill|2019|en|2019|WD=Q25274|lt=2019|1=2019}} (the first year of {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}}){{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}}. Although the burial facilities are unclear due to the leveling of the mounds, many wooden artifacts have been found in excellent condition from the moats – presumably because the filled-in moats retained moisture{{Sfn|四条古墳群（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}. In particular, Tumulus No.1 has revealed its entire structure as a square tumulus with a built‐in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōshutsu|1=Platform-style projection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}) component, with numerous haniwa and wooden artifacts recovered{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. In addition to the tumuli, settlement traces from the Kofun period were also discovered, and together they are collectively referred to as the "Shijō Iseki"{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Near the kofun group lie the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (Misanzai) and the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321|lt=Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}} (Tsukayama), suggesting a possible relationship between these early imperial tombs and the kofun group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. == List == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ List of the Shijō Kofun Group (Bold indicates principal tumulus) {{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|四条遺跡第38次調査 現地説明会資料|2019}} ! Tumulus Name ! Shape ! Scale ! Construction Order ! Construction Period ! Notes |- | '''Tumulus No.1''' || {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|Platform-style projection|ja|造出}} || Mound length: 38 m || 4 || Late Middle to Early Late || Various haniwa and wooden artifacts were excavated |- | '''Tumulus No.2''' || {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|lt=Scallop-shaped Kofun|lt_ja=帆立貝式古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} || Mound length: 42 m || 3 || Late Middle || Various haniwa and wooden artifacts were excavated |- | Tumulus No.3 || {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Side length: 18 m || 5 || Early Late || Haniwa were excavated |- | Tumulus No.4 || {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Diameter: 22.5 m || 1 || Early Middle || |- | Tumulus No.5 || {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Side length: 15–16 m || 4 || Late Middle to Early Late || Wooden artifacts were excavated |- | Tumulus No.6 || {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Diameter: 18 m || 5 || Early Late || Haniwa were excavated |- | '''Tumulus No.7''' || {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} || Diameter: 65 m || 2 || Late Middle || Various haniwa and wooden artifacts were excavated |- | Tumulus No.8 || {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Diameter: 13.5 m || 3 || Late Middle || |- | Tumulus No.9 || {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Diameter: 26 m || 4 || Late Middle to Early Late || Haniwa and wooden artifacts were excavated |- | Tumulus No.10 || {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || Side length: 10.5–12 m || 4 || Late Middle to Early Late || Haniwa were excavated |- | Tumulus No.11 || || || || || |- | Tumulus No.12 || {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || — || 4 || Late Middle to Early Late || Haniwa were excavated |- | Tumulus No.13 || || || || || |- | Tumulus No.14 || {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with {{ill|Platform-style projection|ja|造出}}|| Diameter: 25 m || — || Early Late || Haniwa were excavated |} It is believed that the principal tumuli were established in the order of Tumulus No.7 → No.2 → No.1, and that the peak period of the kofun group was from the latter half to the end of the middle period{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. 四条6号墳出土 家形埴輪片.JPG|Tumulus No.6 – House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photo taken during an exhibition by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}.}} Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 9go-fun.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Explanatory sign 四条9号墳出土 鳥形木製品.JPG|Tumulus No.9 – Wooden artifact in the shape of a bird
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}.}} Shijo Kofun Group (Kashihara)-14 in 2019.jpg|Tumulus No.14 (Excavation survey in 2019) == Tumulus No.1 == [[File:Shijo Kofungun (Kashihara), 1go-fun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Tumulus No.1}}{{small|Photo taken during the 2017 excavation survey.}}]] '''Tumulus No.1''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It was the first tumulus discovered within the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}} ({{Coord|34|30|5.45|N|135|47|29.03|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=四条古墳群1号墳}}), and excavation surveys by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} and others have been conducted since {{ill|1987|en|1987|WD=Q2429|lt=1987|1=1987}}. Although the mound was destroyed and its fill lost during the construction of Fujiwara-kyō, it originally measured approximately 32 meters north–south and 40 meters east–west (including the built-in structure){{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. On the western side of the mound, a trapezoidal built-in ({{ill|Zōshutsu|ja|造出|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōshutsu|1=Platform-style projection|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17221469}}) measuring 9 meters in length and 14 meters in width is present. Two layers of surrounding moats encircle the tumulus, and including these, the total length of the kofun is about 60 meters north–south and an estimated 75 meters east–west{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The inner moat is approximately 6 meters wide and 0.8–1.2 meters deep, while the rampart (the embankment between the inner and outer moats) measures about 6 meters in width{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. Numerous wooden artifacts and {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been excavated from the inner moat; many of these wooden artifacts, including wooden haniwa, are thought to have been used along with haniwa to encircle the tumulus. The outer moat, which reaches a maximum width of approximately 6 meters and a depth of 0.5 meters on the northern side, is shallow and irregular—suggesting it was excavated for the construction of the rampart{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. The burial facility remains unclear due to the leveling. The construction period is estimated to be around the late 5th century of the middle Kofun period{{Sfn|四条遺跡第36次調査 現地説明会資料|2017}}. 四条古墳群 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Many wooden artifacts were detected. Exhibited at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}.}} 四条古墳出土 埴輪 馬.JPG|Haniwa Horse
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}.}} 四条1号墳出土 鹿形埴輪.JPG|Haniwa Deer (Reclining Deer)
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}.}} == Relationship with the Tombs of Emperor Jimmu and Emperor Suizei == {{Multiple image|right|Tomb of Emperor Jimmu, haisho.JPG|Tomb of Emperor Suizei, haisho.JPG|200|{{Center|Top: {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q200188}}
Bottom: {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}}}}} As imperial tombs in close proximity to the Shijō Kofun Group, a hill designated as the tomb of the second Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suizei|en|Emperor Suizei|WD=Q313321|lt=Suizei|1=Emperor Suizei}} (Tsukayama) is located 100 meters south of Tumulus No.7 ({{Coord|34|30|2.71|N|135|47|21.65|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=塚山（綏靖天皇陵）}})), and approximately 350 meters further to the south-southwest is a hill designated as the tomb of the first Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (Misanzai) ({{Coord|34|29|51.04|N|135|47|16.67|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=ミサンザイ（神武天皇陵）}}){{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. However, the current {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q200188}} and {{ill|Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja|綏靖天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suizei Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} are designations from the end of the Edo period, and there has long been a theory that Tsukayama should be identified as the tomb of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. Since both Tsukayama and Misanzai are currently designated as {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Emperor’s tomb|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, the archaeological details remain unclear{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Tsukayama, having a mound approximately 30 meters in diameter, is considered a {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and in that case, it would be one of the constituent tumuli of the Shijō Kofun Group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. Furthermore, Misanzai, in which {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} have been confirmed, might originally have been a kofun and thus possibly a constituent tumulus of the group{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}. According to literature, in the entry for July 23 of the first year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, it is recorded that Emperor Tenmu offered horses and weapons to the tomb of {{ill|Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja|神日本磐余彦天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamukusubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q200188}} – indicating that a tomb for Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} existed even before the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|WD=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō|1=Fujiwara-kyō}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. In general, since Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} is widely regarded as a progenitor king, it is theorized that during the Tenmu period a need arose to create a tomb for the progenitor king, and there is a theory that the pre-existing Misanzai (or Tsukayama) was repurposed without being leveled; another theory relates the creation of the progenitor king’s tomb to the construction plan of Fujiwara-kyō{{Sfn|橿考研|2010}}{{Sfn|神武天皇（古代史）|2006}}. It is also proposed that the current area of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q200188}} was originally the temple grounds of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|1=Ōkubo-dera temple ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683584}} (now a defunct temple). According to this theory, the small hill that existed before the designation of the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q200188}} was a square, mound-like structure, and from the place name "塔垣内" in the surrounding area, it has been suggested that this hill originally formed part of the pagoda base of {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|1=Ōkubo-dera temple ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683584}}.「大窪寺跡」 in {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 30: Place Names of Nara Prefecture, {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}, 1981. There is also a theory that {{ill|Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|ja|大窪寺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkubo-ji†大窪寺跡|1=Ōkubo-dera temple ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28683584}} itself served as the founding temple (or grave-side temple) for the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja|神武天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Jimmu Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神武天皇|1=Emperor Jimmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q200188}}{{ill|Mori Kōichi|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Kōichi|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, 『Invitation to the Imperial Tomb Kofun ({{ill|Chikuma Sensho|ja|筑摩選書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuma Sensho|1=Q17229142|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17229142}}23)』, {{ill|Chikuma Shobō|en|Chikuma Shobō|WD=Q2963556|lt=Chikuma Shobō|1=Chikuma Shobō}}, 2011, pp. 259–263. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute Museum|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} (in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara City|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|Unebayacho|ja|畝傍町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unebayacho|1=Unebi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17174379}}) – Stores and exhibits the artifacts excavated from the Shijō Kofun Group. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Publications by the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}}: ** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.kashikoken.jp/event/atrium/2010/shijyo-site.pdf "Shijō Iseki – Excavation Survey and Its Results Related to the Construction of the Yamato Takada Bypass"]}} ({{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} Atrium Exhibition Materials), 2010.|ref={{Harvid|橿考研|2010}}}} **
